metadata.title: Government Schemes [India & States] Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Which state government has launched the “Mukhya Mantri Pratigya scheme”?
questions.explanation: Recently, Bihar launched the Mukhya Mantri Pratigya scheme to give financial support to youths for internships after Class 12. Youths aged 18–28 with Class 12, Industrial Training Institutes diploma, graduation, or post-graduation will get support for internships lasting 3–12 months. They will get ₹4,000–₹6,000 per month, with extra ₹2,000 for internships outside their district and ₹5,000 outside the state for up to three months. Payment will go directly to bank accounts, monitored by a special committee.

questions.question: Employment Linked Incentive Scheme was announced in which Union Budget?
questions.explanation: Recently, the Union Cabinet chaired by the Prime Minister of India approved the Employment Linked Incentive Scheme. The Employment Linked Incentive Scheme supports employment generation, skill enhancement, and social security in all sectors, with a focus on manufacturing. It was announced in the Union Budget 2024-25 as part of the Prime Minister’s package of five schemes for 4.1 Crore youth with a budget of Rs 2 Lakh Crore. It aims to create over 3.5 Crore jobs in two years, with 1.92 Crore first-time workforce entrants.

questions.question: ‘Niyad Nella Nar’ scheme was launched by which state government?
questions.explanation: Irkabhatti village in Narayanpur district, Bastar division, once lacked basic facilities due to Maoist insurgency. Recently, the Chhattisgarh government improved the situation through the ‘Niyad Nella Nar’ scheme. ‘Niyad Nella Nar’ means “your good village” in the local Dandami dialect of south Bastar. It is a Chhattisgarh government initiative for welfare in Naxal-affected villages. The scheme focuses on Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs). It provides housing, healthcare, water, electricity, roads, and education.

questions.question: Which state government has launched the Digi-Lakshmi scheme to digitally empower urban poor women?
questions.explanation: In June 2025, the Government of Andhra Pradesh launched the ‘Digi-Lakshmi’ scheme to digitally empower urban poor women. A total of 9,034 Common Service Centres (CSCs) will be set up across all Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in the state. These CSCs will be run by eligible Self-Help Group (SHG) women under the ‘One Family, One Entrepreneur’ (OF-OE) vision. The Mission Director of MEPMA (Mission for Elimination of Poverty in Municipal Areas) will implement the scheme.

questions.question: Pradhan Mantri Virasat Ka Samvardhan (PM VIKAS) scheme was launched by which ministry?

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

questions.explanation: A new skill training and women entrepreneurship project under Pradhan Mantri Virasat Ka Samvardhan (PM VIKAS) Scheme launched on 17th July 2025 at Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Kottayam. It aims to train 450 minority candidates in Kerala with future-ready and entrepreneurial skills. PM VIKAS Scheme is a Central Sector Scheme launched by the Ministry of Minority Affairs. It focuses on skill development, leadership, and entrepreneurship training for minorities and artisan communities. The scheme aims to benefit around 9 lakh candidates during the 15th Finance Commission Cycle (2025–26).

questions.question: Which state/UT has launched “One-Time Regularization Scheme” for unauthorised constructions in July 2025?
questions.explanation: On July 22, 2025, Puducherry Chief Minister N. Rangasamy launched the “Puducherry One-Time Regularization Scheme for Unpermitted Constructions and Deviations 2025.” The scheme was inaugurated at the Legislative Assembly in the presence of Speaker R. Selvam, Minister K. Lakshminarayanan, and officials. It applies to buildings constructed and roofed/floored between May 1, 1987, and July 16, 2025, across all Puducherry regions. A special software called Online Building Permission System (OBPS) by National Informatics Centre (NIC) will handle the process. The scheme prevents sealing, demolition, and disconnection of electricity, water, or sewage for violating buildings.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme that allows young Indians to live, work, or study in the UK for up to 2 years?
questions.explanation: The United Kingdom (UK) has opened its final 2025 visa ballot under the India Young Professionals Scheme for Indians aged 18–30 to live, work, or study in the UK for two years. The scheme was part of the India-UK Migration and Mobility Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed in May 2021 and announced during the G20 summit in Bali. It was officially launched in February 2023. It allows Indian citizens aged 18–30 with a bachelor’s degree or higher to live and work in the UK for up to two years.

questions.question: Which state government has launched foreign language scheme named CM-FLIGHT to boost overseas employment?
questions.explanation: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma announced the Chief Minister’s Foreign Language Initiative for Global Human Talent (CM-FLIGHT) to enhance youth employability abroad. The scheme will offer structured training in foreign languages, including up to Japanese Language Proficiency Test (JLPT) N2 level. The project aligns with Japan’s Specified Skilled Worker (SSW) visa program to facilitate paid short-term jobs in Japan. A subsidy of ₹1.5 lakh will be provided to each selected applicant for language training. This initiative aims to tackle unemployment and underemployment in Assam through international job access.

questions.question: The Scheme for Promotion of Culture of Science (SPoCS) is an initiative of which ministry?
questions.explanation: Recently, Union Minister for Culture and Tourism Gajendra Singh Shekhawat shared updates on Scheme for Promotion of Culture of Science (SPoCS) in Rajya Sabha. Scheme for Promotion of Culture of Science (SPoCS) is a flagship scheme of the Ministry of Culture, Government of India. It aims to foster scientific temper and awareness among the public and students. National Council of Science Museums (NCSM) is the nodal agency for implementation. SPoCS supports Science Cities, Science Centres, Innovation Hubs, and Digital Planetariums. As of July 2025, 27 Science Centres have been set up across India. These centres offer hands-on exhibits, workshops, and outreach to promote science.

questions.question: Atal Pension Yojana is regulated by which organization?

options.label: C
options.text: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)

options.label: D
options.text: Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA)

answer.label: D
answer.text: Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA)

questions.explanation: Atal Pension Yojana (APY) has crossed 8 crore enrolments in 2025, marking its 10th anniversary. 39 lakh new subscribers were added in the current financial year. APY is a government-backed pension scheme launched in 2015. It provides social security to workers in the unorganised sector. It is regulated by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA). The scheme guarantees fixed monthly pension after the age of 60. It promotes retirement planning for people without formal pension coverage. It supports inclusive financial security and long-term welfare for low-income workers.
